svg.text: ARABIC + GREEK — RUNNING TEXT · PASSAGES
ALT DRAFT · SERIES 13D · REMEMBER FORWARD
— ARABIC — PASSAGE 1 · SURAH AL-FATIHA (OPENING) —
بِسْمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحْمَنِ الرَّحِيمِ
Bismi llāhi r-raḥmāni r-raḥīm
"In the name of God, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful."
— ARABIC — PASSAGE 2 · PROVERB —
العلم في الصغر كالنقش على الحجر
Al-ʿilmu fiʾṣ-ṣighari kaʾʺn-naqshi ʿalaʾʼ-ḥajar
"Knowledge in youth is like carving on stone." — Early learning endures.
— ARABIC — BRIDGE PHRASE · REMEMBER FORWARD —
هذه الرسالة صُنِعَت لك، مجاناً، من أناسٍ تذكَّروا المستقبَل
Hādhihi r-risālatu ṣuniʿat laka, majjānan, min unāsin tadhakkarū l-mustaqbal.
This message was-made for-you, freely, from people who-remembered the-future.
"This message was made for you, freely, by people who remembered the future."
— LANGUAGE NOTE — "FUTURE" IN ARABIC —
المستقبل al-mustaqbal: lit. "what will be received / what lies ahead to be embraced."
Root q-b-l: to face, to receive. The future is what stands before you, facing you.
إلى الأمام ilā l-amām: "forward" — lit. "toward the front." Amām = in front.
GREEK (ELLINIKΑ) · RUNNING TEXT · PASSAGES
— GREEK — PASSAGE 1 · HERACLITUS —
πάντα ρεί
Pánta reí
"Everything flows." — Heraclitus, ~500 BCE. All things are in constant change.
— GREEK — PASSAGE 2 · PROTAGORAS —
πάντων χρημάτων μέτρον άνθρωπος
Pánton chrēmáton métron ánthrōpos.
"Man is the measure of all things." — Protagoras, ~450 BCE.
— GREEK — BRIDGE PHRASE · REMEMBER FORWARD —
Αυτό φτιάχτηκε για σένα, δωρεάν, από ανθρώπους που θυμήθηκαν το μέλλον.
Afó ftiaxtíke ya séna, doreán, apó antrópous pou thimíthikan to méllon.
This was-made for you, freely, by people who remembered the-future.
"This was made for you, freely, by people who remembered the future."
— LANGUAGE NOTE — "FUTURE" IN GREEK —
μέλλον (méllon): "future" — from μέλλω (méllo) = to be about to, to intend.
The Greek future is grammatically the "about-to-be" — the imminent, the intended.
μπροστά (brostá): "forward" — lit. "in front, ahead." εμπρός (emprós) = to the front.
— DECODING NOTE — FOR FUTURE READERS —
Arabic: 1.9B speakers, official in 22 countries. Right-to-left abjad. See plates 13A–13C for full grammar.
Greek: ~13M speakers (Greece + Cyprus). Left-to-right alphabet. Ancestor of Latin and Cyrillic.
Both languages shaped science, mathematics, and philosophy. Arabic preserved Greek texts 700–1300 CE.
Geographic link: Mediterranean. Cairo to Athens ~2,000 km; to Crete ~1,400 km (within 500mi radius).
PLATE 13D ALT · ARABIC / GREEK · TEXT · CC BY-SA 4.0 · REMEMBERFORWARD.ORG
THIS IS A PATIENT MESSAGE. IT WAS MADE FOR YOU, FREELY, BY PEOPLE WHO REMEMBERED FORWARD.
BUY IT · BUILD IT · BURY IT · FOR SOMEONE YOU WILL NEVER MEET
